title: Holiday Checkout Freeze
impact: Customers could not complete grocery orders for 40 minutes during a holiday sale. Carts showed spinning loaders, support volume spiked, and some customers abandoned orders.
rootCause: The checkout monolith performed cart validation, inventory checks, payment confirmation, order creation, and delivery scheduling in one synchronous path. A traffic spike saturated the database pool.
remediation: FreshCart launched CheckoutFlow, split checkout responsibilities, added order lifecycle events, and created dashboards for checkout latency and event lag.

title: Out-of-Stock Orders
impact: Customers successfully ordered popular items that stores no longer had on shelves. Shoppers had to substitute items, and customer satisfaction dropped.
rootCause: Inventory was checked at the start of checkout but not reserved. Multiple customers could buy the same low-stock item before store systems updated.
remediation: StockSync added inventory reservations during checkout and automatic release rules when orders failed or were cancelled.

title: Duplicate Delivery Notifications
impact: Some customers received the same out-for-delivery notification three or four times. Support had to reassure customers that their order was not duplicated.
rootCause: Delivery notification events were retried after a timeout, but the notification consumer had no idempotency key to recognize repeated messages.
remediation: CheckoutFlow and DeliveryTrack added stable idempotency keys to order and delivery notification events.

title: Stale Inventory Search Results
impact: Customers saw groceries as available in search results even though the store feed had not updated for over an hour. This increased substitutions and cancelled items.
rootCause: StockSync did not expose freshness timestamps, so search and checkout could not tell when a store inventory feed was stale.
remediation: StockSync added freshness timestamps, stale-feed alerts, and support-visible inventory freshness context.
